articles.title: Gains and Losses: Assessing the Trajectories of the War on Iran
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: The three main parties to the conflict—the United States and Israel on one side, and Iran on the other—have achieved certain gains and suffered setbacks, yet none has reached the conclusion that the expected benefits no longer justify the costs involved.
articles.title_cn: 得失权衡：对伊朗战争轨迹的评估

articles.title: Escalation Without Exit: Strategic Disorientation and Potential Scenarios in the US–Israeli War on Iran
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: More than a month into the US-Israeli war on Iran, early assumptions of rapid victory have unravelled. Strategic ambiguity, escalation dynamics and resilient Iranian responses point to a protracted conflict with regional spillovers, infrastructure targeting and rising global economic risks, with no clear end-state.
articles.title_cn: 升级无出口：US-Israeli对伊朗战争的战略迷茫与可能情景

articles.title: Iran’s Strategic Options: Rethinking Negotiation with America
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: Iranian realism rejects reliance on US promises, emphasising deterrence, domestic strength and tangible power. Historical US treaty breaches, unilateral withdrawals, and interventionism justify Iran’s pragmatic strategy: conditional diplomacy, active deterrence and prioritised national security over formal agreements.
articles.title_cn: Iran战略选择：重新思考与America的谈判

articles.title: Secularism and Sacred Narratives: The Contradictions of US Political Rhetoric in the 2026 Iran War
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: The US presents itself as a secular democracy, yet its wartime rhetoric frequently incorporates moral and religious language. The 2026 US-Israeli war on Iran highlights a structural tension between formal secularism and the practical reliance on religious narratives to justify and sustain military action.
articles.title_cn: 世俗主义与神圣叙事：2026年Iran战争中US政治言辞的矛盾

articles.title: The War No One Can Win: Why Shattering the Iranian Regime Is a Strategic Miscalculation
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: The collapse of the Iranian regime risks catastrophic horizontal war and political collapse, with Israel as the region’s main existential threat. US policies dismantling its own system push the Middle East back into 19th-century power politics, ending the brief ‘Dengist era’ and its connectivity projects.
articles.title_cn: 无人能赢的战争：摧毁Iranian政权是战略误判

articles.title: The Strait of Hormuz: Global Economic Shock and the Limits of Military Power
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: Reopening the Strait of Hormuz may be militarily possible, but it would likely be costly and time-consuming for the United States. Even attempting it could trigger a global economic shock. Persistent threats to shipping could take weeks or months to suppress, making long-term control impractical.
articles.title_cn: Hormuz海峡：全球经济冲击与军事力量的局限

articles.title: Strategic Paralysis and Escalatory Spillover: Applying Warden’s Five Ring Theory in Operation Epic Fury
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: Operation Epic Fury, launched 28 February 2026 by the US and Israel, targets Iran’s leadership and critical systems to induce systemic collapse under Warden’s Five Ring Theory. Iran retaliates against Gulf infrastructure, sparking a high-stakes clash with global energy and regional security implications.
articles.title_cn: 战略瘫痪与升级溢出：Operation Epic Fury中Warden五环理论的应用

articles.title: The Iran War Unraveling: Expanding Scope and Ambiguous Objectives
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: One week after its outbreak, the Iran war has expanded in scope and intensity, with rising losses, more targets and increasingly diverse weapons. The warring parties still believe persistence will achieve their aims, and the conflict has not yet reached a point where the costs of continuing outweigh a ceasefire.
articles.title_cn: Iran战争瓦解：范围扩大与目标模糊

articles.title: Blinding US Eyes in the Middle East
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: Iran is targeting US-aligned missile-defence infrastructure in the ongoing conflict, striking early-warning radars and THAAD sites across the Middle East. These attacks aim to degrade regional detection and interception capabilities supporting the broader US–Israel missile-defence network.
articles.title_cn: 蒙蔽US在Middle East的眼睛

articles.title: Strategic Escalation and Conflict Sustainability in the US-Iran War
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: Escalation in the US-Iran war hinges less on battlefield dominance than on endurance. While Washington relies on high-intensity decapitation strikes, Tehran pursues decentralised attrition and energy leverage, shifting the decisive variable toward sustainability, regional spillover and political resilience.
articles.title_cn: 美伊战争中的战略升级与冲突持续性

articles.title: Reviving India-Oman Relations: Strategic  Cooperation and Economic Integration
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: India and Oman’s centuries-old ties, reinforced by a large Indian diaspora, have evolved into a strategic partnership spanning trade, energy, defence and connectivity. Modi’s 2025 visit and the CEPA trade deal deepen economic integration, maritime security and green energy cooperation, amid regional rivalry and China’s growing presence.
articles.title_cn: 复兴印度-阿曼关系：战略合作与经济一体化

articles.title: Targeting the Regime: The Second Round of the US–Israeli War on Iran
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: Israel and the US launched a new round of war against Iran, concentrating their attacks on the regime’s leadership. They assassinated Supreme Leader Khamenei, struck key state command-and-control centres, called for the emergence of new leadership prepared to accept their terms, and at the same time urged Iranians to overturn their country’s regime.
articles.title_cn: 针对伊朗政权：美以战争第二轮

articles.title: AJCS Publishes New Book on Small States in an Era of International Conflict
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies has released a new book titled Dhil al-Qaysar: ad-Duwal as-Saghira fi Zaman as-Sira‘ ad-Duwali [The Tsar’s Shadow: Small States in an Era of International Conflict], by Abdullah Rashid Al-Mursel Al-Marri. Spanning 365 pages, the book examines how the Russia-Ukraine war has reshaped the positions of small and peripheral states within the international system, revealing new roles that have emerged on the margins of the conflict, far from the centre of direct confronta
articles.title_cn: AJCS发布国际冲突时代小国研究新著

articles.title: On the Brink of War: US and Iranian Calculations in the New Negotiations
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: The United States and Iran conducted a new round of negotiations amid US preparations to strike Iran and demands for greater concessions than before.
articles.title_cn: 战争边缘：美伊新谈判中的战略计算

articles.title: Methodological Approaches to Investigative Journalism and Their Impact on Enhancing Its Quality
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: This study examines methodological approaches in investigative journalism and their intersections with scientific research methods, exploring how these frameworks can be employed to enhance the quality of investigative reporting. It aims to provide a comprehensive understanding of the methodological tools used in this field by addressing a central research question: What is the most effective methodology for producing high-quality investigative journalism, and how does it contribute to the devel
articles.title_cn: 调查性新闻学的方法论与质量提升影响

articles.title: Why Trump Hesitates to Go to War with Iran
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: Trump refrains from war with Iran, where any conflict would be prolonged, costly and unpredictable. Iran’s enduring strategy, regional reach and missile capabilities make diplomacy and targeted strikes the only realistic path, highlighting high-stakes geopolitical calculations.
articles.title_cn: 特朗普对伊朗用兵的犹豫原因

articles.title: Proceedings of the 17th Al Jazeera Forum: The Palestinian Cause and the Regional Balance of Power in the Context of an Emerging Multipolar World
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: The 17th Al Jazeera Forum was held in Doha from 7 to 9 February 2026, with participation from a diverse group of policymakers, researchers, experts and media professionals. The forum focused on the Palestinian cause within the context of rapid regional and international transformations.
articles.title_cn: 第17届半岛电视台论坛：多极化新兴时代的巴勒斯坦问题与地区力量平衡

articles.title: Iran’s Internal Challenges and Confrontations Abroad
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: Strikes began in Tehran’s bazaar over currency fluctuations and spread to other cities amid inflation and falling purchasing power. After ten days of peaceful protests for better living conditions, violence rarely witnessed in the country erupted, with slogans targeting the regime, national security and stability.
articles.title_cn: 伊朗的内部挑战与对外对抗

articles.title: Damascus’s Approach to The SDF Question: A Shift in the Path Toward Unifying Syrian Territory
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: Damascus’s approach to the SDF marks a pivotal step toward unifying Syria’s territory. By combining political and military measures, the government dismantled the SDF’s legitimacy, strengthened state control, and set a clear path for integration, producing significant local, regional and international consequences for Syria’s reconstruction.
articles.title_cn: 大马士革对叙利亚民主力量问题的态度：统一叙利亚领土的路径转变

articles.title: Lubab Explores Power Shifts in an Emerging International System in Its 29th Issue
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: The 29th issue of Lubab Journal for Strategic Studies was released in early February 2026. It examines transformations in power, competition and protest within an emerging international system, against a backdrop of rapidly accelerating structural changes at the international, regional and national levels.
articles.title_cn: Lubab第29期探讨新兴国际体系的权力转变

articles.title: Developments in Northeastern Syria: The End of the SDF?
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: Within days, control in northeastern Syria shifted dramatically. Clashes in Aleppo between state forces and the SDF escalated into a swift Syrian army advance, retaking Deir Az Zor and Raqqa and reaching Ayn al-Arab and Hasakah, raising questions about the end of the SDF-led rebellion since 2014.
articles.title_cn: 叙利亚东北部发展动态：叙利亚民主力量的终结

articles.title: 17th Al Jazeera Forum to Explore Shifts in the Palestinian Cause in a Multipolar World
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: From 7 to 9 February 2026, Doha will host the 17th Al Jazeera Forum under the title, “The Palestinian Cause and the Regional Balance of Power in the Context of an Emerging Multipolar World”. The event will bring together decision-makers, researchers, experts and journalists from across the globe.
articles.title_cn: 第17届半岛电视台论坛：多极化世界中的巴勒斯坦事业转变

articles.title: A Dual Dilemma: Political and Economic Erosion of Mali’s Military Leadership
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: Military and political pressure on Mali’s ruling junta is growing. It can no longer fully control energy and food supplies to the capital or suppress all political threats, forcing it to tighten its security grip while seeking alternatives to offset shortages.
articles.title_cn: 双重困境：马里军事领导层的政治与经济侵蚀

articles.title: Escalation in Eastern Yemen and the Test of the Saudi-Emirati Partnership: Regional Implications Beyond the Yemeni Arena
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: Military escalation in Hadramout and Al-Mahra marked a clear shift in how Yemen's conflict is managed, bringing the Saudi-Emirati rift into the open. This development fits into a wider regional context, reflecting a recurring pattern whereby regional powers reshape balances of power by empowering local armed actors.
articles.title_cn: 也门东部升级与沙特-阿联酋伙伴关系考验：也门外的地区影响

articles.title: US National Security Strategy: The Trump Administration’s Vision for the United States and the World
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: Trump's 2025 National Security Strategy marks a shift from post-Cold War US policy, rejecting global hegemony for "America First" realism. It prioritises sovereignty, economic security, reduced intervention, and burden-sharing with allies while focusing on containing China and hemispheric dominance.
articles.title_cn: 美国国家安全战略：Trump政府对美国和世界的愿景

articles.title: Tehran’s “Day Zero”: A Threat or an Opportunity to Revive Water Governance and Adaptive Water Policy Making in Iran?
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: “Day Zero” is the moment when residents of a city or region no longer have access to drinking water—when the last drop flows from their taps. First coined in Cape Town, South Africa, (1) this term is now being echoed in 2025 from Tehran, a capital with over 15 million inhabitants. Concerns about the sustainable provision of drinking water in Tehran and other major Iranian cities have reached critical levels.
articles.title_cn: Tehran的"零日"：伊朗水资源治理和适应性水政策的威胁还是机遇？

articles.title: AJCS and Katara Discuss the Role of Culture and Art in Supporting Freedom and Achieving Justice
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies, in collaboration with the Cultural Village Foundation – Katara, hosted an extensive intellectual seminar on Thursday, 25 December 2025, titled “The Role of Culture and Art in Supporting Freedom and Achieving Justice”, featuring distinguished group of intellectuals, researchers and media professionals.
articles.title_cn: AJCS和Katara讨论文化和艺术在支持自由和实现正义中的作用

articles.title: In the Wake of the War on Gaza: Shifts in the Balance of Power in the Middle East
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: Israel weakened its enemies after the October 2023 attacks but did not defeat them or turn its military actions into a strategic advantage, as it had hoped. Meanwhile, Syria and Turkey regained influence, and the expansion of Arab-Israeli normalisation stalled.
articles.title_cn: Gaza战争后：中东力量均衡的转变

articles.title: Twelve Days of Inferno: The Cost of Opening Pandora’s Box
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: In June 2025, Israel launched Operation Rising Lion, striking Iran’s nuclear, missile and civilian sites. Iran retaliated with 500+ ballistic missiles over 12 days. The conflict inflicted severe civilian and infrastructure damage, challenged Israel’s missile defences, and revealed strategic reserves on both sides.
articles.title_cn: 地狱的十二天：打开潘多拉魔盒的代价

articles.title: Bahrain’s cabinet announces a timely economic support package
